commit_scope: Loosen the kernel fanout boundary by making Rust and Go bridge traffic carry a native Form/BML invitation while preserving fanout semantics.

commands: cd form/form-kernel-rust && cargo fmt --check && cargo build --release && python3 test_serve.py
local_validation.summary: Rust and Go fanout bridges now write X-Form-Native-Invitation headers on fanout responses and upstream requests. The headers name native-invitation-offered, Form/BML route recipe, selected path fanout-python, native_invitation_declined, and X-Form-Python-Fallback. Router-owned headers are filtered before forwarding/relaying so clients or upstreams cannot clobber the bridge decision.

known_limitations.observed: This release does not promote any additional handler to native execution. X-Form-Router and selected path remain fanout-python for unpromoted traffic.
Fanout response bodies, status codes, content type, and streaming/buffered error semantics remain unchanged.

ci_validation.notes: Pending push, PR checks, merge, and production verification.

deploy_validation.notes: Public deploy verification will run after merge/deploy. This bridge contract is primarily local kernel runtime behavior and does not add a new public route.

e2e_validation.expected_behavior_delta: A request that falls through to Python is still explicitly non-native, but it now receives and forwards an offer to speak the Form/BML route recipe next time. Explicit Python fallback remains the visible decline/control signal.
public_endpoints: Any kernel-router fanout response marked X-Form-Router: fanout-python
test_flows: Rust test_serve.py proved successful fanout, upstream 404 fanout, method fallthrough, and buffered upstream failure carry the invitation headers.
Go TestFanoutForwardsBodyAndMarksBridge proved response and upstream request invitation headers, including client/upstream clobber filtering.

phase_gate.reason: Local proof is complete. CI, merge, and post-deploy verification remain before claiming this live.
